docs.title: PowerPoint Components
docs.text:
Build presentation slides with reusable Python components and a builder-style layout flow.

Design-System Feel

Reusable components with predictable spacing, hierarchy, and theme tokens.

API-First Docs

Function signatures and class docs rendered directly from source with mkdocstrings.

Visual Validation

Dark and light slide previews included so docs stay practical for presentation work.

docs.title: Why this docs site
docs.text:
• Component-level API reference generated directly from code.
  • Searchable docs for quick discovery.
  • Practical examples tuned for presentation design workflows.

docs.title: Start Here
docs.text:
• Browse Text Box for text-focused components.
  • Browse Chart for chart and data-visualization APIs.
  • Browse Visual Gallery for dark/light output examples.
  • Use Component Reference for a broad manual lookup.

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
python -m venv .venv
.venv\Scripts\activate
python -m pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: Local Docs Preview
docs.text:
mkdocs serve

Open http://127.0.0.1:8000

docs.title: Dark And Light Preview
docs.text: Dark theme: timeline, comparison cards, and chart composition. Light theme: same composition with a neutral presentation surface.

docs.title: Visual Source Notes
docs.text:
This folder is reserved for future checked-in docs visuals.

Current docs examples reference stable image URLs from the repository's exported demo slides: - examples/demo_dark_slides - examples/demo_light_slides

docs.title: Components Documentation
docs.text: This folder contains GitHub-friendly docs for all presentation components in this repository.

docs.title: Navigation
docs.text:
• Visual Gallery
  • Component Reference
  • Composition Patterns
  • Module Catalog

docs.title: Public vs Internal Imports
docs.text:
Most components are available from top-level pptx_components imports:

import pptx_components as pc

A few components currently exist in the codebase but are not re-exported at the package root. Import those from module paths directly:

from pptx_components.components.timeline import Timeline
from pptx_components.components.comparison import ComparisonPanel

docs.title: Minimal Slide Pattern
docs.text:
from pptx import Presentation
import pptx_components as pc

prs = Presentation()
pc.set_theme(pc.DarkTheme())

b = pc.SlideBuilder(prs)
b.add(pc.SectionHeader("Status"))
b.skip(0.1)
b.add_row(
    pc.MetricCard("Revenue", "$1.2M", delta="+18%", delta_positive=True),
    pc.MetricCard("Users", "24,370", delta="+7%", delta_positive=True),
    pc.MetricCard("Churn", "3.2%", delta="+0.4pp", delta_positive=False),
    h=1.5,
)

prs.save("example.pptx")

docs.title: Composition Patterns
docs.text: This page shows practical patterns for composing slides with SlideBuilder.

docs.title: 1) Executive Dashboard Pattern
docs.text:
import pptx_components as pc

b.add(pc.TitleBlock("Executive Dashboard", "Weekly operating metrics"))
b.skip(0.1)
b.add_row(
    pc.MetricCard("MRR", "$128K", delta="+12%", delta_positive=True),
    pc.MetricCard("NPS", "72", delta="+4pts", delta_positive=True),
    pc.MetricCard("Churn", "3.2%", delta="+0.4pp", delta_positive=False),
    h=1.5,
)
b.skip(0.15)
b.add(pc.BarChart(["Jan", "Feb", "Mar"], {"Revenue": [90, 98, 105]}), h=2.4)

docs.title: 2) Status And Risk Pattern
docs.text:
b.add(pc.SectionHeader("Operational Status", badge_text="WEEK 24"))
b.skip(0.1)
b.add(pc.CalloutBox("Payment retry latency above target in EU region.", style="warning"))
b.add(pc.CalloutBox("API error rate stabilized after hotfix.", style="success"))
b.skip(0.1)
b.add(pc.ProgressBar("API v3 rollout", 62))
b.add(pc.ProgressBar("Infrastructure migration", 40))

docs.title: 3) Product Story Pattern With Navigation
docs.text:
b.add(
    pc.TabsPanel(
        ["Overview", "Analytics", "Risks", "Decisions"],
        active_index=1,
        title="Q2 Review",
        variant="line",
        content="Activation improved by 6% and CAC dropped 8%.",
    ),
    h=1.9,
)
b.skip(0.15)
b.add(
    pc.StepFlow(
        ["Discovery", "Prototype", "Validation", "Launch", "Scale"],
        current=2,
        statuses=["done", "done", "current", "pending", "pending"],
    ),
    h=1.4,
)

docs.title: 4) Data + Legend + KPI Grid Pattern
docs.text:
legend_items = [
    ("APAC", (59, 130, 246)),
    ("EMEA", (16, 185, 129)),
    ("Americas", (249, 115, 22)),
]

b.add_row(
    pc.ImageBlock("examples/demo_dark_slides/slide_004.png", mode="contain"),
    pc.Legend(legend_items, title="Region Color Legend"),
    h=2.5,
    weights=[1.6, 1.0],
)
b.skip(0.1)
b.add(
    pc.KPIGrid(
        [
            ("New Leads", "1,284", "+9%", True),
            ("Conversion", "14.2%", "+1.1pp", True),
            ("Win Rate", "31%", "-2pp", False),
            ("Sales Cycle", "27 days", "-3 days", True),
        ],
        cols=2,
    ),
    h=2.2,
)

docs.title: 5) Internal Components Pattern
docs.text:
If you want to use components not yet re-exported at package root:

from pptx_components.components.timeline import Timeline
from pptx_components.components.comparison import ComparisonPanel

b.add(
    Timeline(
        [
            ("Jan", "Discovery", "done"),
            ("Feb", "Prototype", "done"),
            ("Mar", "Validation", "current"),
            ("Apr", "Launch", "upcoming"),
        ],
        title="Roadmap",
    ),
    h=1.8,
)

b.add(
    ComparisonPanel(
        "Before",
        ["Manual reporting", "Siloed dashboards", "Late alerts"],
        "After",
        ["Automated pipeline", "Unified metrics", "Near real-time alerts"],
        title="Transformation",
    ),
    h=2.2,
)

docs.title: Component Reference
docs.text: This reference documents constructor parameters and common usage snippets.

docs.title: Precedence
docs.text:
Theme resolution follows this order:

  1. Explicit component-level theme passed to render(..., theme=...).
  2. Local section override applied by Container(local_theme=..., theme_patch=...).
  3. SlideBuilder(theme=...) scope.
  4. Global fallback from set_theme(...) / get_theme().

docs.title: Container Section Override API
docs.text:
Import: pc.Container

Signature additions:

Container(
    child: Component,
    padding: float | None = None,
    fill_rgb: tuple[int, int, int] | None = None,
    border_rgb: tuple[int, int, int] | None = None,
    radius: float = 0.05,
    local_theme: Theme | None = None,
    theme_patch: Mapping[str, object] | None = None,
)

theme_patch lets you patch only selected theme tokens for a section subtree. Supported keys include spacing/typography constants and semantic color tokens:

  • DISPLAY, HEADING, SUBHEADING, BODY, CAPTION
  • XS, SM, MD, LG, XL
  • SLIDE_W, SLIDE_H, MARGIN
  • BG, SURFACE, SURFACE_ALT
  • TEXT_PRIMARY, TEXT_SECONDARY, TEXT_MUTED
  • ACCENT, ACCENT_SOFT, CALLOUT, POSITIVE, NEGATIVE

Example:

section = pc.Container(
    pc.Column(
        pc.MetricCard("Pipeline", "$3.4M", "+6%", True),
        pc.TextCard("Section note", "This block uses a warmer local surface."),
    ),
    theme_patch={
        "SURFACE": (255, 248, 236),
        "SURFACE_ALT": (246, 230, 206),
        "TEXT_PRIMARY": (68, 44, 22),
        "MD": 0.18,
    },
)

docs.title: React Mental Model Mapping
docs.text:
• SlideBuilder(theme=...) is analogous to ThemeProvider scope.
  • Container(local_theme=..., theme_patch=...) is analogous to nested provider overrides.
  • render(..., theme=...) is analogous to per-component explicit prop override.
  • set_theme(...) is analogous to process-level default context value.

docs.title: Chart
docs.text: Data-visualization components for bars, lines, pie, and scatter patterns.

docs.title: Visual Example
docs.text:
Dark theme composite with horizontal bar comparison. Light theme variant with consistent geometry and spacing.

Tip: Start with BarChart(mode="bar_clustered") for decision matrices and ScatterChart for relationship-heavy analysis slides.

Use stacked bar chart. Deprecated — use mode= instead.

Optional explicit chart mode. Supported values: "column_clustered" (default), "column_stacked", "column_stacked_100", "bar_clustered", "bar_stacked".

docs.title: pptx_components.components.scatter.ScatterPlot
docs.text:
Bases: Component

XY scatter plot visualization for correlation and distribution analysis.

Renders data points as circles on a 2D grid. Useful for: - Correlation analysis (feature vs. performance) - Outlier detection - Risk/return quadrants - Portfolio positioning

Axes
  • X-axis: Horizontal numeric range
  • Y-axis: Vertical numeric range
  • Optional quadrant dividers and labels
  • Configurable axis labels and tick marks
Data Points
  • Each point: (x, y, size=0.15, color=theme.ACCENT)
  • Optional point labels (text annotation)
  • Optional grouping by color

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default points list[tuple[float, float, str | None, tuple[int, int, int] | None, float]]

List of (x, y, label=None, color_rgb=None, size=0.15) tuples. x, y: Numeric coordinates (will be scaled to grid). label: Optional text label for point. color_rgb: Optional (R, G, B) point color; theme.ACCENT used if None. size: Point radius in inches. Default: 0.15 (small dot).

required x_label str | None

X-axis label string (e.g. "Features", "Cost").

None y_label str | None

Y-axis label string (e.g. "Performance", "Value").

None title str | None

Optional plot title.

None x_range tuple[float, float] | None

(min, max) numeric range for x-axis. Auto-calculated if None.

None y_range tuple[float, float] | None

(min, max) numeric range for y-axis. Auto-calculated if None.

None show_grid bool

Whether to draw gridlines. Default: True.

True quadrant_labels tuple[str, str, str, str] | None

(top_left, top_right, bottom_left, bottom_right) text labels for quadrants.

None Example

scatter = ScatterPlot( ... points=[ ... (2.5, 8.0, "Algorithm A", (100, 200, 255), 0.2), ... (5.0, 6.5, "Algorithm B", (100, 200, 255), 0.2), ... (3.0, 4.0, "Outlier", (255, 100, 100), 0.25), ... ], ... x_label="Complexity", ... y_label="Accuracy (%)", ... title="Model Performance Landscape", ... quadrant_labels=("High Perf", "Ideal", "Low Complexity", "Avoid") ... )

docs.title: pptx_components.components.waterfall.WaterfallChart
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Cumulative delta (waterfall) chart built from themed rectangles.

Each bar floats at the cumulative level, showing a positive (POSITIVE color) or negative (NEGATIVE color) contribution. An optional Total bar spans from zero to the final sum and is rendered in ACCENT color.

No external chart library required — uses pptx rectangles directly.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default categories list[str]

Label for each delta step.

required values list[float]

Positive or negative change at each step.

required title str | None

Optional heading above the chart.

None show_total bool

Append a summary "Total" bar.

True total_label str

Label for the total bar.

'Total'

docs.title: Components Guide
docs.text: This section contains practical notes for using the component system effectively.

docs.title: Public vs Internal Imports
docs.text:
Most components are available from top-level imports:

import pptx_components as pc

Some components may be imported directly from module paths when not re-exported:

from pptx_components.components.timeline import Timeline
from pptx_components.components.comparison import ComparisonPanel

docs.title: Minimal Slide Pattern
docs.text:
from pptx import Presentation
import pptx_components as pc

prs = Presentation()
pc.set_theme(pc.DarkTheme())

builder = pc.SlideBuilder(prs)
builder.add(pc.SectionHeader("Status"))
builder.skip(0.1)
builder.add_row(
    pc.MetricCard("Revenue", "$1.2M", delta="+18%", delta_positive=True),
    pc.MetricCard("Users", "24,370", delta="+7%", delta_positive=True),
    pc.MetricCard("Churn", "3.2%", delta="+0.4pp", delta_positive=False),
    h=1.5,
)

prs.save("example.pptx")

docs.title: Text Box
docs.text: Text-focused components for narrative surfaces, headers, and structured copy.

docs.title: Visual Example
docs.text:
List and narrative-heavy layout in dark theme. Equivalent light theme layout for reports and executive decks.

Tip: Use NarrativeTwoColumnPage and LongNarrativeBlock when content length is variable and needs pagination-safe rendering.

docs.title: pptx_components.components.text_card.TextCard
docs.text:
Bases: Component

General-purpose surface card for narrative text and body copy.

A flexible rectangular text panel with an optional title, left accent bar, and rounded corners. Three styles control the surface and text palette.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default body str

The main body copy to display.

required title str | None

Optional heading rendered above the body in bold.

None style str

"default" | "muted" | "accent"

'default'

docs.title: pptx_components.components.title.TitleBlock
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Full-width title with optional subtitle and top accent bar.

docs.title: pptx_components.components.title.SectionHeader
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Horizontal section break with left accent bar and optional right-aligned badge.

docs.title: pptx_components.components.narrative.NarrativePage
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Framed long-form narrative panel with page metadata and key takeaways.

docs.title: pptx_components.components.narrative.NarrativeTwoColumnPage
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Long-form narrative layout with a right-side evidence or takeaway rail.

docs.title: pptx_components.components.narrative.LongNarrativeBlock
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Auto-paginated narrative component for long-form slide storytelling.

Use paginate_narrative(...) if you want to pre-generate all pages, or pass page to render one page at a time.

docs.title: pptx_components.components.narrative.paginate_narrative
docs.text:
paginate_narrative(
    text: str,
    max_chars_per_page: int = 700,
    max_paragraphs_per_page: int = 3,
) -> list[str]

Split long narrative text into slide-friendly pages.

Paragraphs are preserved when possible and oversized paragraphs are sentence-split before word-level fallback.

docs.title: pptx_components.components.narrative.build_narrative_slides
docs.text:
build_narrative_slides(
    prs,
    title: str,
    text: str,
    summary: str | None = None,
    key_points: list[str] | None = None,
    sidebar_title: str | None = None,
    sidebar_points: list[str] | None = None,
    sidebar_note: str | None = None,
    max_chars_per_page: int = 700,
    max_paragraphs_per_page: int = 3,
    theme: Theme | None = None,
) -> list

Generate one slide per narrative page and return created slide objects.

docs.title: Module Catalog
docs.text:
Browse every component module in the library.

Each page is generated with mkdocstrings so function signatures and docstrings stay synced with code.

docs.title: Annotation
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Floating note box with an optional directional pointer.

Inspired by Tooltip / Popover patterns in shadcn/ui and MUI.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default text str

Annotation message.

required style str

"note" | "highlight" | "warning" | "info"

'note' pointer str | None

Direction the pointer triangle sticks out: "top" | "bottom" | "left" | "right" | None.

'bottom' title str | None

Optional bold heading above the body text.

None

docs.title: CalloutBox
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Styled notification box with semantic color and a bold prefix label.

Color pairs (fill, text) come from theme.CALLOUT — no color logic here.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default text str

The message to display.

required style str

"info" | "warning" | "success" | "error"

'info'

docs.title: Divider
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Horizontal rule with optional centered label.

docs.title: Spacer
docs.text:
Bases: Component

No-op component that occupies vertical space in a Column.

docs.title: GroupedTable
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Table with group headers, sub-rows, and optional group footers.

Organizes hierarchical data into collapsible groups and rows. Useful for: - Regional/departmental breakdowns with totals - Category hierarchies (Product > SKU > Variant) - Org charts with team members - Budget hierarchies (Program > Project > Line Item)

Layout
  • Group header row (bold, colored background)
  • Data rows (indented, lighter background)
  • Optional group footer row (summary, subtle color)
  • Column headers at top
Data Structure

groups: List of { "header": "Group Name" or ("Group Name", value1, value2, ...), "rows": [ ("Item 1", val1, val2, ...), ("Item 2", val1, val2, ...), ], "footer": ("Total", val1, val2, ...) or None, }

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default columns list[str]

List of column header strings (e.g. ["Region", "Q1", "Q2", "Q3"]).

required groups list[dict]

List of group dicts as described above.

required title str | None

Optional table title.

None column_widths list[float] | None

List of width fractions; auto-calculated if None.

None show_dividers bool

Whether to show group separators. Default: True.

True Example

table = GroupedTable( ... columns=["Region", "2025", "2026", "+/- YoY"], ... groups=[ ... { ... "header": "NORTH AMERICA", ... "rows": [ ... ("USA", "$500M", "$620M", "+24%"), ... ("Canada", "$80M", "$95M", "+19%"), ... ], ... "footer": ("SubtotalNA", "$580M", "$715M", "+23%"), ... }, ... { ... "header": "EMEA", ... "rows": [ ... ("UK", "$200M", "$245M", "+22%"), ... ("France", "$150M", "$180M", "+20%"), ... ], ... "footer": ("SubtotalEMEA", "$350M", "$425M", "+21%"), ... }, ... ], ... title="Regional Revenue Review", ... )

docs.title: Heatmap
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Grid-based intensity heatmap.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default matrix list[list[float]]

2-D list of floats (rows × cols).

required row_labels list[str]

Labels for each row.

required col_labels list[str]

Labels for each column.

required title str | None

Optional heading above the heatmap.

None colormap str

"sequential" (SURFACE_ALT → ACCENT) or "diverging" (NEGATIVE → SURFACE_ALT → POSITIVE).

'sequential' show_values bool

Render the numeric value inside each cell.

True fmt str

Python format spec string applied to each value (default "g").

'g'

docs.title: ImageBlock
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Render an image inside a bounding box.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default image_path str

Path to the source image.

required mode str

"contain" (preserve ratio in box), "stretch" (fill box), "fit_width" (full width), or "fit_height" (full height).

'contain' border_rgb tuple[int, int, int] | None

Optional border color tuple.

None border_width_pt float

Border width when border_rgb is provided.

1.0

required cols int

Number of columns.

docs.title: ListBlock

Unified list component supporting bullet, numbered, and checklist styles.

"bullet" | "number" | "check"

docs.title: BigStat
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Hero statistic — large centered number for single-focus slides.

docs.title: MetricCard
docs.text:
Bases: Component

KPI card with label, value, and optional delta indicator.

Renders a compact metric display with accent bar, value, and optional directional delta. Useful for dashboards, executive summaries, and data-driven storytelling.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default label str

The metric name (e.g. "Revenue").

required value str

The formatted value string (e.g. "$1.2M"). Caller owns formatting.

required delta str | None

Pre-formatted change string (e.g. "+18%", "-5.2%"). Optional.

None delta_positive bool | None

Sentiment direction — True=positive (green), False=negative (red), None=neutral.

None Example

builder.add(MetricCard("Revenue", "$1.2M", "+18%", True)) builder.add(MetricCard("Churn", "3.1%", "+0.2pp", False))

docs.title: LongNarrativeBlock
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Auto-paginated narrative component for long-form slide storytelling.

Use paginate_narrative(...) if you want to pre-generate all pages, or pass page to render one page at a time.

docs.title: NarrativePage
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Framed long-form narrative panel with page metadata and key takeaways.

docs.title: NarrativeTwoColumnPage
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Long-form narrative layout with a right-side evidence or takeaway rail.

docs.title: build_narrative_slides
docs.text:
build_narrative_slides(
    prs,
    title: str,
    text: str,
    summary: str | None = None,
    key_points: list[str] | None = None,
    sidebar_title: str | None = None,
    sidebar_points: list[str] | None = None,
    sidebar_note: str | None = None,
    max_chars_per_page: int = 700,
    max_paragraphs_per_page: int = 3,
    theme: Theme | None = None,
) -> list

Generate one slide per narrative page and return created slide objects.

docs.title: paginate_narrative
docs.text:
paginate_narrative(
    text: str,
    max_chars_per_page: int = 700,
    max_paragraphs_per_page: int = 3,
) -> list[str]

Split long narrative text into slide-friendly pages.

Paragraphs are preserved when possible and oversized paragraphs are sentence-split before word-level fallback.

docs.title: AccordionBlock
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Collapsible accordion inspired by MUI/shadcn Accordion.

For slides, one item remains expanded; others render collapsed headers. Useful for FAQ, nested sections, or hiding verbose details.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default items list[tuple[str, str]]

List of (label, content) tuples.

required expanded_index int

Which item is open (0-based). Others show collapsed.

0 title str | None

Optional heading above the accordion.

None allow_all_closed bool

If True, no header gets the expanded state.

False

docs.title: FeatureGrid
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Feature/benefit showcase grid inspired by modern landing pages.

Arranges features in a grid with icon area, title, and description. Practical for showcasing product highlights or capability matrix.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default features list[tuple[str, str, str]]

List of (icon_char, title, description) tuples.

required columns int

Grid columns (typically 2 or 3).

3 title str | None

Optional heading above grid.

None

docs.title: StepFlow
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Horizontal stepper inspired by UI libraries such as Ant Design Steps.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default steps list[str]

Ordered step labels.

required current int

Active step index.

0 statuses list[str] | None

Optional explicit status list per step: "done" | "current" | "pending" | "error". If omitted, status is inferred from current.

None title str | None

Optional heading above the stepper.

None show_numbers bool

Show step numbers for non-completed states.

True

docs.title: TabsPanel
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Static tabs-style panel inspired by React Tabs components.

This is intentionally non-interactive for slides: one active tab is rendered with its associated body text while inactive tabs remain as headers.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default tabs list[str]

Ordered tab labels.

required active_index int

Which tab appears selected.

0 content str | None

Optional body text to show for the active tab.

None title str | None

Optional heading above tabs.

None variant str

"pill" | "line"

'pill'

docs.title: ProgressBar
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Horizontal progress bar with label and optional percentage.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default label str

Description shown on the left.

required value float

Current value.

required max_value float

Maximum value (default 100).

100 show_pct bool

Show percentage on the right side.

True

docs.title: StatusBadge
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Fixed-size pill badge. Anchors to top-left of bounding box.

Designed to be composed inside other components rather than used standalone in add_row(). Fixed intrinsic size: 1.2" wide × 0.3" tall.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default text str

Badge label.

required status str

"ok" | "warn" | "error"

'ok'

docs.title: RangeIndicator
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Multi-band threshold bar with a current-value marker.

Inspired by MUI's LinearProgress + Gauge composition pattern.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default label str

Description of the metric.

required value float

Current value to mark on the bar.

required segments list[tuple[float, str, str]]

Ordered list of (upper_bound, band_label, status) tuples. The first band starts at min_value. The last upper_bound becomes the scale maximum. status: "ok" | "warn" | "error"

required min_value float

Minimum of the scale (default 0).

0.0

docs.title: ScatterPlot
docs.text:
Bases: Component

XY scatter plot visualization for correlation and distribution analysis.

Renders data points as circles on a 2D grid. Useful for: - Correlation analysis (feature vs. performance) - Outlier detection - Risk/return quadrants - Portfolio positioning

Axes
  • X-axis: Horizontal numeric range
  • Y-axis: Vertical numeric range
  • Optional quadrant dividers and labels
  • Configurable axis labels and tick marks
Data Points
  • Each point: (x, y, size=0.15, color=theme.ACCENT)
  • Optional point labels (text annotation)
  • Optional grouping by color

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default points list[tuple[float, float, str | None, tuple[int, int, int] | None, float]]

List of (x, y, label=None, color_rgb=None, size=0.15) tuples. x, y: Numeric coordinates (will be scaled to grid). label: Optional text label for point. color_rgb: Optional (R, G, B) point color; theme.ACCENT used if None. size: Point radius in inches. Default: 0.15 (small dot).

required x_label str | None

X-axis label string (e.g. "Features", "Cost").

None y_label str | None

Y-axis label string (e.g. "Performance", "Value").

None title str | None

Optional plot title.

None x_range tuple[float, float] | None

(min, max) numeric range for x-axis. Auto-calculated if None.

None y_range tuple[float, float] | None

(min, max) numeric range for y-axis. Auto-calculated if None.

None show_grid bool

Whether to draw gridlines. Default: True.

True quadrant_labels tuple[str, str, str, str] | None

(top_left, top_right, bottom_left, bottom_right) text labels for quadrants.

None Example

scatter = ScatterPlot( ... points=[ ... (2.5, 8.0, "Algorithm A", (100, 200, 255), 0.2), ... (5.0, 6.5, "Algorithm B", (100, 200, 255), 0.2), ... (3.0, 4.0, "Outlier", (255, 100, 100), 0.25), ... ], ... x_label="Complexity", ... y_label="Accuracy (%)", ... title="Model Performance Landscape", ... quadrant_labels=("High Perf", "Ideal", "Low Complexity", "Avoid") ... )

docs.title: DataTable
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Tabular data with accent header row, zebra stripes, and proportional columns.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default headers list[str]

Column header strings.

required rows list[list[str]]

List of rows; each row is a list of cell strings.

Proportional column widths (e.g. [3,1,1]). Defaults to equal.

None zebra bool

Alternate row background colors.

True accent_header bool

Use accent color for the header row.

True

docs.title: TextCard
docs.text:
Bases: Component

General-purpose surface card for narrative text and body copy.

A flexible rectangular text panel with an optional title, left accent bar, and rounded corners. Three styles control the surface and text palette.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default body str

The main body copy to display.

required title str | None

Optional heading rendered above the body in bold.

None style str

"default" | "muted" | "accent"

'default'

Full-width title with optional subtitle and top accent bar.

docs.title: WaterfallChart
docs.text:
Bases: Component

Cumulative delta (waterfall) chart built from themed rectangles.

Each bar floats at the cumulative level, showing a positive (POSITIVE color) or negative (NEGATIVE color) contribution. An optional Total bar spans from zero to the final sum and is rendered in ACCENT color.

No external chart library required — uses pptx rectangles directly.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default categories list[str]

Label for each delta step.

required values list[float]

Positive or negative change at each step.

required title str | None

Optional heading above the chart.

None show_total bool

Append a summary "Total" bar.

True total_label str

Label for the total bar.

'Total'
